subject: [POLICY] add env passwords to api/pap/xacml/dist
commit_message:
[POLICY] add env passwords to api/pap/xacml/dist

keystore and truststore passwords are now stored as
secrets to be accessed by environment variables.

Issue-ID: POLICY-2575
Signed-off-by: jhh <jorge.hernandez-herrero@att.com>
Change-Id: Ie74dc74e53d5f2a39a7c9771d32b43e537819e47
Signed-off-by: jhh <jorge.hernandez-herrero@att.com>

Patch Set 2:

Jorge, seems like we need to release the latest images to take advantage of this? Perhaps we make this WIP until that happens after the next sprint.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> 
> @Jorge
> Thank you for your work, it looks awesome but... For G release we want to mandate (requirement is already submitted, waiting for TSC vote next week) all projects to use certInitializer and retrieve their certificates at runtime then you would not need those changes right?

Ah, ok, thanks, I was not aware of it.
I have a question though.  If we get the certificates at runtime, those certificates will end up in the java keystore (truststore) in the JVM within the container. Do we care about administering the passwords that the JVM's keystore/truststore passwords with external configuration?   If we do, this work may still be relevant in the context on setting the passwords for the keystore/truststore, otherwise we can just abandon the review.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > @Jorge
> > Thank you for your work, it looks awesome but... For G release we want to mandate (requirement is already submitted, waiting for TSC vote next week) all projects to use certInitializer and retrieve their certificates at runtime then you would not need those changes right?
> 
> Ah, ok, thanks, I was not aware of it.
> I have a question though.  If we get the certificates at runtime, those certificates will end up in the java keystore (truststore) in the JVM within the container. Do we care about administering the passwords that the JVM's keystore/truststore passwords with external configuration?   If we do, this work may still be relevant in the context on setting the passwords for the keystore/truststore, otherwise we can just abandon the review.

So the password ends up in a config file in the tmpfs volume so we don't care to much about that as it's never (apart from swap but we don't consider it here) written to the disk nor transferred anywhere

Patch Set 2:

I will abandon this one shortly, cherry pick and submit only to frankfurt, I think there is no contraint on certInitializer there.

Abandoned

certInitalizer requirement
